program_chi: 雙渠道雙語研究文學士
learning_outcomes:
BMBL 1. Major
Programme: Upon
completing the programme, students will have: 1. Achieved a new understanding of how knowledge of language is
organized and transmitted through the visual-spatial modality, as opposed to
spoken languages, organized and transmitted in fundamentally different ways; 2. Appreciated how this new understanding can potentially
transform the society’s perception about sign language, giving it a linguistic status that is equal
to spoken languages; 3. To be able to evaluate critically
the development of bimodal bilingualism in society from the perspective of
information accessibility and effective communication in domains of
education, social and public communication, child care, counselling and
clinical services; and 4. To be proficient in spoken languages and sign languages in
order to undertake responsibilities in professional settings which call for
such linguistic skills.
study_scheme:
Bimodal Bilingual Studies Bimodal Bilingual Studies Applicable to students admitted in 2025-26
Major
Programme Requirement (for Associate Degree or Higher Diploma holders)
Students
are required to complete a minimum of 51 units of courses as follows:

Courses:
(a)  |  Foundation
Courses: BMBL1001,
1002  |  6
(b)  |  Sign
Linguistics Courses: BMBL2001,
2002, 2003, 2004  |  12
(c)  |  Hong
Kong Sign Language Courses: (i) HKSL1003 (ii) HKSL2003 (iii) HKSL3001 and 3002, or HKSL3003  |  6 6 6
2.  |  Elective
and Capstone Courses:
(a)  |  Any two
courses from: BMBL2005,
3002, 3003  |  6
(b)  |  Two
courses from either (i) or (ii):  |  3
(i)  |  BMBL4101
and 4102 (capstone courses – research)
(ii)  |  BMBL4201
and 4202 (capstone courses – service learning)
(c)  |  Any 6 units of courses from the following five areas of study:  |  6
(i)  |  Area
I: Linguistics
BMBL4000,
LING1000, 1001, 1002, 1003, 2003, 2004, 2005, 2006, 2301, 3101, 3403
(ii)  |  Area

Total:  |  51
Explanatory
Note:
1.  |  BMBL,
CHED, EDUC, HKSL, LING, SLPA, SOWK and TRAN courses at 2000 and above
level as included in the Major Programme Requirement will be included in
the calculation of Major GPA for honours classification.
2.  |  Subject
to the Department’s approval, students can take the following HKSL
courses to substitute the courses specified in 1(c): (i)
HKSL1000 and 1002 to substitute HKSL1003; (ii)
HKSL2001 and 2002 to substitute HKSL2003.
3.  |  For
students whose native language is Hong Kong Sign Language (HKSL) or who
possess equivalent competence in HKSL, subject to the approval of the
Major Department, they should take either an option of (a) and/or (b), or
a combination of (a) to (c) to fufill the Major Programme Requirements: (a)
courses for a modern language other than English, Chinese and HKSL; (b) courses
listed under the above study scheme, and/or courses prefixed LING, with
not more than six units of courses at the level of 1000; (c) up
to three units of BMBL4000 taken in the first three terms of study. The
exemption arrangements are specified below:
Units
of HKSL Courses Exempted  |  Course
Substitution

(b) listed above
